information: XUL component framework: 37 total components. Base wrappers in xul/base/ (30 classes like Toolbar, Button, Browser), composed at root (SidebarMain, WebPanelButton, etc.). All extend base classes. Composition pattern: WebPanelButton contains WebPanelSoundIcon + NotificationBadge. Registered with CustomizableUI for drag-drop customization.

Command palette: VS Code-style command palette integrated via UrlbarProvider API. Entry: command_palette.uc.mjs. Features: fuzzy search (exact match 200pts, prefix 100+, initials 90+, word boundaries +15, consecutive +20), 50+ static commands, dynamic commands (about: pages, search engines, extensions, containers). Prefs: command-palette.prefix (default \":\"), command-palette.max-commands (3), command-palette.min-score-threshold (150). User config in chrome/command-palette-settings.json. Original by BibekBhusal0 for Zen Browser.

Quick fix checklist: 1) Check Browser Console (Ctrl+Shift+J) for errors, 2) Verify prefs (toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets=true, svg.context-properties.content.enabled=true), 3) Clear startup cache (about:support → Clear startup cache), 4) Test minimal theme (disable scripts one-by-one), 5) Check file permissions (should be 644), 6) Verify fx-autoconfig (config.js in Firefox install dir).

Custom preferences (about:config): firefoxcss.tabs.on.top.enable, firefoxcss.pocket.*, sidebar.verticalTabs, sidebar.revamp, svg.context-properties.content.enabled (REQUIRED for icons), mod.search.info.buttons.show.always, mod.hide.*, userChromeJS.* (fx-autoconfig), userChrome.tabs.all-tabs-menu.reverse-order, userChrome.ctrlTab.skip-show-all-button, command-palette.* (16+ prefs). See .documentation/preferences.md for full list.

second_sidebar.uc.mjs entry point: Checks for sb2-webpanels-window class (skip if present), calls ContextualIdentityServiceWrapper.ensureDataReady(), then SidebarInjector.inject(). On success: SidebarDecorator.decorate() → CustomizeModePatcher.patch(). Waits for UC_API.Runtime.startupFinished() or delayedStartupPromise.

userChrome.css structure: 167 lines of pure @imports. CRITICAL import order: 1) common/variables.css (foundation), 2) common/colors.css (theme), 3) chrome/*.css (components). NO actual styles in entry file - all modular. Heavily documented with ASCII art separators explaining each import's purpose/dependencies.

CSS architecture: Import order matters - common/variables.css first, then common/colors.css, then components. Heavy use of CSS custom properties (--firefoxcss-* prefix for theme, --uc-* for components). Heavy !important usage to override Firefox defaults. NO preprocessors (SASS/LESS).

Settings architecture: Two storage mechanisms. 1) Firefox Preferences (Services.prefs) - JSON-serialized objects in prefs.js. Keys: second-sidebar.* (sidebar config), command-palette.* (palette behavior), firefoxcss.* (CSS toggles). 2) JSON Files - IOUtils for file I/O, chrome/command-palette-settings.json for user customizations. Settings base class pattern: static load(pref)/save(pref, value) with JSON.parse/stringify.

Project scale: 311 files total, ~22,000 lines of code. Second sidebar subsystem alone: 156 ES6 modules, ~12,607 lines. 15 standalone .uc.js scripts. 27 CSS files. Max directory depth: 6 levels (intentional module isolation). 12 files >500 lines.

CSS pain points: Margin inconsistencies common (many refactor commits), icon rendering issues frequent, tabs behavior bugs. Cleanup needed regularly (unused styles accumulate). Color consistency requires maintenance. Use Browser Toolbox (Ctrl+Alt+Shift+I) to debug CSS live.

Static Factory Pattern: Classes use static create() for complex initialization with side effects. Example: SidebarElements.create() creates all components and registers them with Firefox. Single instance per window, global access via static properties.

Documentation: 16 markdown files. Key docs: installation.md, configuration.md, preferences.md, troubleshooting.md, development.md, architecture.md, css-components.md, css-variables.md, javascript-modules.md, userchrome-js.md, compatibility.md, COMMAND_PALETTE_CONFIG.md. Multiple AGENTS.md files (root, JS/, second_sidebar/, xul/, CSS/).

Standalone UserChrome.js scripts (15 total): extendedStatusbar.uc.js (statusbar with link preview), restart_button.uc.js (restart button, Ctrl+Shift+R), pdfTools.uc.js (PDF.js enhancements), tabContextMenuNavigation.uc.js (tab menu enhancements), minimizeMemory.uc.js (memory optimization), rebuild_userChrome.uc.js (rebuild CSS), plus 9 more. Each self-contained, can be removed individually.

chrome.manifest: Maps chrome:// URLs to local paths. content userchromejs ./ (root), content userscripts ../JS/ (scripts), skin userstyles classic/1.0 ../CSS/ (legacy), content userchrome ../resources/ (assets), content userchromecss ../css/ (modern CSS). Located at utils/chrome.manifest.
Fluent API: All setter methods return 'this' for method chaining. Example: new SidebarMain().setMode("icons").setContext("menu").setAttribute("customizable", "true").appendChild(button)

File naming: snake_case.uc.js for standalone scripts, snake_case.uc.mjs for ES6 entry points, snake_case.mjs for internal modules, kebab-case.css for stylesheets. Examples: restart_button.uc.js, second_sidebar.uc.mjs, toolbar-order.css

Second sidebar system: 100+ file ES6 module implementing Vivaldi/Floorp-style web panels. Entry: second_sidebar.uc.mjs → SidebarInjector → SidebarElements (37 XUL components) → SidebarControllers (21 controllers) → Settings. Subsystems: xul/ (UI), wrappers/ (28 Firefox API wrappers), controllers/ (21 feature controllers), utils/ (10 utilities), patchers/ (4 Firefox patches), settings/ (8 storage), css/ (12 modules), icons/ (2 utilities).

Wrapper pattern: 28 wrapper classes in JS/second_sidebar/wrappers/ abstract Firefox internals. Benefits: type safety (JSDoc), simplified APIs, version compatibility, testing capability. Example: PreferencesWrapper wraps Services.prefs, CustomizableUIWrapper wraps CustomizableUI. Legacy .uc.js scripts use direct API access, modern modules use wrappers.

Performance overhead: +40-100MB memory usage depending on platform. Web panels accumulate memory if not closed. Blur effects and animations can cause lag on slower systems. Fix: Use minimizeMemory.uc.js script, disable effects by setting --enable-blur-effects: 0 and --animation-duration: 0ms in CSS.

Development activity: 150 commits in past year, 21 in past month. Active maintenance. Main contributor: Thomas Leon Highbaugh (various name variants, 572+ commits total). NO automated tests - manual verification only. NO formal build system. Changes require manual Firefox restart.
Branching strategy: master (main development), imperative (contains install.sh automation script), imgbot (image optimization). Development happens on master directly.

Firefox version requirements: 100+ (tested on Stable, ESR, Beta, Nightly). Platforms: Windows 10/11, macOS 10.15+, Linux (most distros). CRITICAL: Must set toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets=true in about:config for CSS to work.

CSS custom properties: --firefoxcss-* prefix for theme (--firefoxcss-main-bg-color, --firefoxcss-tab-hover-bg-color, --firefoxcss-border-radius, --firefoxcss-toolbar-button-*), --uc-* for components. Light/dark via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark|light). Heavy !important usage to override Firefox defaults. XUL namespace declared. Firefox selectors: #TabsToolbar, #navigator-toolbox, .tabbrowser-tab, #urlbar-container, #PersonalToolbar.

Testing strategy: Manual verification only. NO automated tests, NO CI/CD. Browser Toolbox (Ctrl+Alt+Shift+I) for live CSS inspection, Browser Console (Ctrl+Shift+J) for JS errors. Disable scripts by renaming .uc.js to .uc.js.disabled. Clear startup cache via about:support if issues.

Known bugs: space_and_separator_restorer.uc.js - CRITICAL: Do not move spaces, flexible spaces, or separators to configuration toolbar (causes glitches). Do not move main 'space'-item to palette (hidden until customization mode reopened). Blank tab workaround implemented, init delay workaround available. See script lines 26, 168.

Tech stack: Pure Firefox userChrome project. NO npm dependencies except .opencode tooling. Requires fx-autoconfig (https://github.com/MrOtherGuy/fx-autoconfig) for JavaScript functionality. Uses ES6 modules (.mjs), UserChrome.js scripts (.uc.js), and CSS3. No build system - Firefox loads files directly from profile's chrome/ directory.

Project scale metrics: 368 total code files (JS/MJS/CSS). 51,070 total lines of code. Breakdown: ~3,965 lines in 15 standalone .uc.js scripts, ~12,607 lines in second_sidebar/ ES6 modules (156 files), rest in CSS modules and command palette. 16 markdown documentation files. ~82 resource files (icons/textures/wallpapers).

Standalone scripts: 15 .uc.js files (3965 lines total). Key scripts: extendedStatusbar (statusbar+link preview, Ctrl+/), restart_button (Ctrl+Shift+R), rebuild_userChrome (rebuild CSS), minimizeMemory (memory optimization), pdfTools (PDF.js), sidebarWidth (remember widths), space_and_separator_restorer (toolbar customization - BUG: don't move spaces to config toolbar), tabContextMenuNavigation, openLinkInUnloadedTab, privateWindowHomepage, allTabsMenuExpansionPack. Each self-contained, removable individually.

CRITICAL gotcha: svg.context-properties.content.enabled MUST be true or theme icons won't display. Shows warning at top of browser if not enabled. Also required: toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets=true for CSS loading. Check Browser Console (Ctrl+Shift+J) for errors. Clear startup cache via about:support if issues.

Commit convention: Simple timestamp format "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" (e.g., "2026-02-03 08:38:36"). NO conventional commits. Occasional "Revert" and descriptive messages. Main contributor: Thomas Leon Highbaugh (272 commits).

Platform-specific: Windows (window controls need adjustment on Win11, blur depends on version, font rendering varies). macOS (sidebar may overlap traffic light buttons - needs margin adjustment, control positioning varies, CSS behaves differently). Linux (font rendering depends on system config, window decorations vary by DE/WM, GTK theme conflicts possible esp. Elementary OS, some distros have modified Firefox packages).

Class naming patterns: *Wrapper suffix for Firefox API wrappers (CustomizableUIWrapper), *Controller for feature controllers, *Patcher for runtime Firefox patches, *Injector for component injection, PascalCase for all classes. Methods: getX()/setX() with setters returning 'this' for chaining, #privateName for private methods/fields.

Module architecture: Entry Point (.uc.mjs) → Injector → Elements Registry (static properties) → Controllers Registry → Load Settings → Apply. Example: second_sidebar.uc.mjs loads SidebarInjector which creates SidebarElements and SidebarControllers.

Resources: ~82 files in resources/ organized into icons/ (UI navigation, Firefox branding, window controls, permission indicators - camera/mic/screen SVG+PNG, light/dark variants), texture/ (lightnoise.png overlay), notifications/statusbar/ (permission icons). Note: icons/icons/ subdirectory appears to be backup. Single wallpaper.jpg. Mostly SVG (scalable) with PNG fallbacks.

Build/Deploy: NO formal build system. NO webpack, bundlers, or compilation. Development workflow: 1) Edit files in chrome/ directory, 2) Restart Firefox (Ctrl+Shift+R or restart_button.uc.js), 3) Manual verification only (no automated tests). Use Browser Toolbox (Ctrl+Alt+Shift+I) for live CSS debugging.

Firefox API usage: 148+ Services calls (Services.prefs, Services.obs, Services.io, Services.wm/ww, Services.dirsvc, Services.appinfo, Services.search, Services.scriptSecurityManager). 81 Ci interfaces (nsIFile, nsIFileURL, nsIStyleSheetService, nsIChromeRegistry, nsIClipboardHelper, nsIWebProgressListener, nsIMemoryReporterManager). 22 Cc instantiations. 23 ChromeUtils calls (importESModule, compileScript, defineLazyGetter, generateQI). 32 CustomizableUI calls. 76 gBrowser references.

Installation: Manual copy to [profile]/chrome/ OR automated via install.sh (on 'imperative' branch, Linux only, supports stable/dev/beta/nightly/esr/wolf variants, auto-detects profile, backs up existing). NixOS has declarative integration (use imperative branch for non-declarative).

Compatibility: Firefox 100+ required. Nightly may break with updates (experimental). Backdrop-filter limited on older ESR versions. XUL elements change between versions. Extension conflicts possible (toolbar positioning). Fix: disable extensions one-by-one. Firefox <90 not supported (major compatibility issues).

Repository: https://github.com/Thomashighbaugh/firefox. First commit: 2021-12-23. Most recent: 2026-02-03. Active development: 4+ years, 150 commits past year, 21 past month. Origin: aminought/firefox-second-sidebar for second sidebar, BibekBhusal0 for command palette. Main dev: Thomas Leon Highbaugh.
